product_updates.title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
product_updates.content: While AI is helping coders produce code faster, it may not be producing better code, researchers warn. And that could cause problems down the road for them.

product_updates.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
product_updates.content: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

product_updates.title: What happens when companies become too AI-pilled?
product_updates.content: The people deciding that AI can replace your job are also the ones least likely to understand what your job truly involves, according to Box founder Aaron Levie, who pointed to this as an example of “AI psychosis.” Indeed, ClickUp recently cut 22% of its workforce for AI agents, tech layoffs in 2…

product_updates.title: After Nvidia’s $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
product_updates.content: Chipmaker Groq is looking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it pivots from hardware to focus more on AI inference, the process of refining the way AI models respond to prompted requests, per Axios.

product_updates.title: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
product_updates.content: Cognition makes Devin, the first and arguably most successful AI coding agent. But famed coder Wu says it isn't designed to supplant human programmers.

product_updates.title: Today is the last day to apply to speak at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
product_updates.content: Submit your session topic before today ends for a chance to speak at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Apply now to share your insight and help shape the conversations defining the tech industry.

product_updates.title: Final 24 hours to save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
product_updates.content: You now have until tonight at 11:59 p.m. PT to lock in Early Bird savings of up to $410 for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 before prices increase. Join 10,000+ tech leaders in October for one of the most anticipated tech events of the year. Register now.

product_updates.title: Kiwibit’s AI-powered bird feeder is my new backyard buddy
product_updates.content: If you're looking for a fun way to connect with nature while collecting bird species on an app like Pokémon, give this smart feeder a try.

product_updates.title: This chip startup just raised $135M on a bet that AI’s biggest bottleneck isn’t compute — it’s memory
product_updates.content: South Korean chip startup XCENA is betting that AI's real bottleneck is not compute, but memory.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
technical_breakthroughs.content: The botnet was reportedly tied to a Russia-based residential proxy network.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The deadly Ebola outbreak is proving difficult to control
technical_breakthroughs.content: The alert was raised on May 5. Four health-care workers in the Ituri Province of the Democratic Republic of the Congo had died from an unknown illness within four days. Rapid response teams were sent to investigate, and tests at a research center in Kinshasa revealed the culprit: the Bundibugyo v…

technical_breakthroughs.title: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
technical_breakthroughs.content: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral.” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity as we e…
